title: Product Authentication
objectives: Explain how GS1 identifiers enable authentication

sections.markdown: **Product authentication** uses GS1 identifiers (GTIN + serial) plus additional signals (crypto tokens, one-time codes, tamper evidence) so a consumer or supply-chain actor can verify a product is genuine and hasn't been diverted.

rows: Verifiable offline with a public key
Detects clones (both scan the same code)

sections.code:
Simple scan-to-verify flow:
  scan QR (Digital Link) -> resolver returns 
    { gtin, serial, brand, first-scanned-at, last-scanned-at, status }
  App logic:
    - Unknown serial      -> FAKE (not issued)
    - Serial already used -> LIKELY CLONE (already scanned as sold)
    - Fresh serial        -> OK; server marks it as consumed

sections.markdown: The clever bit: serials are **first-sale tokens**. The first scan after purchase is expected; subsequent scans of the same serial suggest either the buyer re-scanning (fine) or a clone with the same printed code (not fine). Server-side state turns a static barcode into a one-shot proof.
Relying only on 'is this GTIN valid?' Counterfeiters copy GTINs trivially. Real authentication needs a UNIQUE per-item element (serial + server state, or a crypto signature).

Combine a Digital Link QR (public, human-readable-ish) with a **server-signed short opaque token** so even without network the app can partially verify, and with network the server can catch clones and enforce first-scan policies.

flashcards.front: Why is GTIN alone weak for authentication?
flashcards.back: GTIN is public; anyone can print the same number.

flashcards.front: How does a serial catch clones?
flashcards.back: Server tracks 'first scan' — a second unrelated device scanning the same serial signals a duplicate.
Digital + tamper-evident physical (holograms, seals) + server state + optional crypto.

options: Cloned duplicates of the same serial

quiz.explain: Clear consumer signal + actionable next step.

challenges.text: Design a JSON response for authentic vs suspicious vs unknown.
Implement server 'first-scan' state with abuse detection.
Add signed short tokens verifiable offline with a rotating key.
Design an anti-cloning strategy combining Digital Link + tamper-evident seals + server-side heuristics (geo/time).

interviewQuestions: How does a serial defeat clones?
How to combine physical + digital signals?
How would you architect a scan-to-verify app?
